# Sensei

> AI Development Intelligence — capture how your team uses AI and feed that intelligence back into your workflow.

## Overview

Sensei is a local-first code intelligence platform that indexes your repositories, tracks AI coding session metrics, and provides context-aware tools to AI coding assistants via MCP (Model Context Protocol).

## Components

- senseid: Indexer daemon. Watches repos, builds a code graph in SQLite, serves HTTP API on port 7744.
- sensei: CLI for installation, configuration, scanning, and daemon management.
- sensei-mcp: MCP server binary. Translates MCP tool calls into senseid HTTP requests. Connects AI platforms like Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Install

```sh
brew install mizukisu/tap/sensei
sensei configure
sensei install
sensei start
sensei scan ~/Developer
```

Or download binaries from: https://github.com/mizukisu/sensei-releases/releases

## CLI Commands

- sensei configure — Detect AI coding platforms and generate config
- sensei install — Install hooks, skills, and MCP server for all configured platforms
- sensei install --acp claude-code — Install for a specific platform
- sensei uninstall — Remove sensei from all platforms
- sensei start — Start the senseid daemon (default port 7744)
- sensei stop — Stop the daemon
- sensei status — Show daemon version, PID, uptime
- sensei scan <path> — Scan a folder and index all repos
- sensei add-lib <name> — Add a library's documentation (auto-discovers llms.txt)

## Daemon Commands

- senseid — Start in foreground
- senseid start — Start in background
- senseid stop — Stop background daemon
- senseid status — Show version and PID
- senseid logs — Tail the daemon log

## MCP Tools (available to AI coding assistants)

- get_session_context(task_description) — Resume from last checkpoint, load repo orientation
- context_pack(query) — Load symbols relevant to a query (token-budgeted)
- search(query) — Semantic search across the codebase
- load_context(file_path) — Load a file with symbol extraction
- take_snapshot(progress_summary) — Record progress for interruption recovery
- checkpoint(outcome, summary) — Close session with FTR scoring

## Architecture

AI Platform (Claude Code / Cursor / Windsurf)
  → sensei-mcp (MCP stdio adapter)
    → senseid (HTTP API on :7744)
      → SQLite graph database
      → File watcher (per-repo)
      → Task queue (scan → repo → folder → file → resolve → connect)

## Key Features

- First-Try-Right scoring: Track how often AI completes tasks on first attempt
- Cost intelligence: Token-level cost tracking per task, session, and repo
- Context packing: BM25 + semantic search for token-budgeted context loading
- Session continuity: Automatic crash recovery and progress snapshots
- Doc drift detection: Traceability matrix linking code to docs
- Plugin ecosystem: Skills, commands, and hooks for Claude Code

## Data

All data stored locally at ~/.sensei/. Nothing sent to external servers.

## Links

- Source: https://github.com/mizukisu/sensei
- Releases: https://github.com/mizukisu/sensei-releases
- Homebrew: brew install mizukisu/tap/sensei
